title: COS Studio-Level Asset Library
description: Cross-project memory. Distinct from a single project's Project Memory (see assets/ufdm-schema-template.json): entries here are promoted, never written directly, per Rule 12 and Rule 9. Owned operationally by Asset Librarian; within-project counterpart is Asset Manager (references/agent-roster.md). v3.1: promotedBy and integrations.ownerAgent moved into required (const pins were unenforced), libraryVersion semver pattern added — per lint_worldforge.py findings.

libraryVersion.description: Semver for the library file itself, bumped on any promotion/demotion.

characters.description: Locked character canon promoted from a project's Character Designer output.

environments.description: Locked environment/location canon promoted from Environment Designer output.

doctrine.description: Studio-level brand/visual/tonal doctrine. Read by Brand Guardian to check any deliverable regardless of which Guild produced it.

colorSystem.description: Named hex values, e.g. from Brand & Identity Guild or a Film Bible's color script.

voiceCanon.description: Locked AI-voice-model + direction pairs per character, owned by Voice Director within a project, promoted here for cross-project reuse.

uiSystems.description: Reusable UI component systems or 3D interactive patterns promoted from Web/Interactive, UI & 3D Studio work (references/web-3d-studio.md), per that file's 'Relationship to Asset Library' section.

lessonsLearned.description: Studio-level lessons from Stage 9 (Learning), Research Agent findings general enough to apply beyond one project, and Business Operations Lead process fixes. NOT process SOPs — those belong to Knowledge Librarian's separate store, kept out of this file on purpose (see references/business-ops-guild.md).

sourceAgent.description: Which agent's finding this originated from, e.g. 'Research Agent', 'QA / Continuity Supervisor'.

integrations.description: External API/plugin connections registered by Plugin/Integration Engineer (references/business-ops-guild.md). Additive only — per that file's 'Plugin architecture principle,' an entry here should never require a change to the Stage Pipeline, UFDM schema, or Guild definitions to function.

purpose.description: What this plugs into and which agent/Guild consumes it, e.g. 'live analytics data for Growth Strategist'.

modelRoutingHistory.description: Studio-level record of model deprecations/switches (Rule 11), so a new project's Model Router check starts from institutional memory rather than zero.

decisionLedger.description: Studio-level decisions with cross-project consequence — every promotion (Rule 12), every tie broken by Creative Director under Rule 13. Project-local decisions stay in that project's own UFDM decisionRecords; only decisions that change what future projects inherit get logged here.

promotedAsset.description: Any asset promoted from a project's Project Memory into studio-level canon. Never written silently — promotedBy/decisionRecordId are mandatory so a promotion is always traceable to the Rule 12 decision that authorized it.

fingerprint.description: The locked fingerprint checked against every new batch per references/asset-fingerprinting.md — a hash, embedding reference, or canonical description, depending on what Asset Manager's within-project tooling produces.

decisionRecordId.description: Foreign key into decisionLedger — the Rule 12 promotion decision. Required; a promotedAsset with no matching decision record is a schema violation, not just a style issue.

projectsUsingThisAsset.description: Reuse tracking — which projects currently reference this asset, e.g. sharing a character between two projects in the same world.

positions.description: One entry per party to a disagreement, for tie-break records — the 'state both positions in one line each' step in Rule 13, kept rather than smoothed over.

resolvedBy.description: Almost always 'Creative Director' per Rule 13, but recorded explicitly rather than assumed.
